name: Germany

authority.name: Bundesnetzagentur (BNetzA) — German eIDAS supervisory body and Trusted List operator
authority.operator: ~15 accredited QTSPs (Bundesdruckerei/D-Trust, Deutsche Telekom / Telekom Security, Bundesnotarkammer, Atos, dgnservice, Bank-Verlag/BVtrust, medisign/MESIG, GD Netcetera, SIGN8, Utimaco, Deutsche Post, Governikus, exceet, and others)

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the German Trusted List (TL-DE.XML, Bundesnetzagentur)

relatedLinks.label: EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: Germany's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (Bundesdruckerei/D-Trust, Deutsche Telekom / Telekom Security / Telesec, Bundesnotarkammer, Atos, dgnservice, Bank-Verlag/BVtrust, medisign/MESIG, GD Netcetera, SIGN8, Utimaco, Deutsche Post, Governikus, exceet, and others) supervised by the Bundesnetzagentur and published in the German national eIDAS Trusted List (TL-DE.XML). The 101 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the German TSL's signature (signer: CN=German Trusted List Signer 14, O=Federal Network Agency, C=DE) against the identity the LOTL declares for Germany, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Note that the national eID root behind the German identity card (Personalausweis), operated by the BSI, is part of the eID-notification mechanism rather than the QTSP Trusted List and is therefore not included in this set.
